advanced_tables.title: Advanced tables
advanced_tables.subtitle: Complex data presentation.
advanced_tables.p1: This section demonstrates some of the more advanced features of Vuetify's data tables.

ai.title: AI / LLMs
ai.subtitle: Adding LLM tools.
ai.p1: This section shows how to add custom data sources to LLMs.

charts.title: Charts
charts.subtitle: Visualizing data with charts.
charts.p1: This section shows some types of charts using Chart.js.

entity_relationships.title: Entity relationships
entity_relationships.subtitle: Managing relationships between entities.
entity_relationships.p1: This section examines the implications of an entity hierarchy: parent, regular, and child/data entities.

external_data.title: External data
external_data.subtitle: Importing and managing external data sources.
external_data.p1: This section shows data from the European Central Bank's API and the MaxMind API.
external_data.p2: In the backend, the it shows how to merge data efficiently using hash tables and how to stream from file directly into the database.

parallel_processing.title: Parallel processing
parallel_processing.subtitle: Improved data throughput.
parallel_processing.p1: This section shows an implementation of job workflows, where each step is a fake backend command to manipulate data.
parallel_processing.p2: When a job is started, each step that can be executed in parallel runs concurrently, and structures are in place to manage failure gracefully.

project_stack.title: Project stack and repositories
project_stack.subtitle: Modern technologies with custom open source libraries on top.
project_stack.p1: This project uses {PostgreSQL} for the database, {Go} on the server, and {VueJs} with {Vuetify} components in the UI.
project_stack.p2: The LoveYourStack open source repositories relevant to this project are:

p2_list.item_3: {connectors} for the ECB and MaxMind connectors.
p2_list.item_4: {lysVue} for the UI library, which is available via {npm}.

project_stack.p3: The cards below give an overview of the subheadings in the left and right navigation panels.

saas.title: SaaS / multi-tenancy
saas.subtitle: Solving SaaS on the data level.
saas.p1: This section shows how to use Postgres RLS (Row-Level Security) to implement multi-tenancy in a SaaS application.
saas.p2: It shows how to access the data both from an internal database connection, and also from a tenant application using a special RLS-enabled database connection.

server_push.title: Server push
server_push.subtitle: Real-time notifications and updates.
server_push.p1: This section demonstrates how data is pushed from database to app (via listen/notify) and from app to frontend (via websockets).

type_handling.title: Type handling
type_handling.subtitle: Dealing with different data types.
type_handling.p1: This section gets the basics out the way; it demonstrates how to handle common data types (e.g. strings, numbers, dates, arrays).
type_handling.p2: It examines the implications of mandatory vs optional vs default values.

user_data_retention.title: User data retention
user_data_retention.subtitle: Techniques for preserving user data.
user_data_retention.p1: This section demonstrates how to properly archive (soft delete) data including cascading effects, and how to track user changes using a database audit log.
